free_text: Eng.transl.of YF,8,(2),286,1968
Data given
Energy and mass distribution of fragments from fission
of U-235 by monoenergetic neutrons from 0. to 15.5 MeV
Van de Graaff
Proton-tritium reaction for neutron energy
interval 0.28-3.5 MeV
- for neutron energy interval 5-7 MeV
- for En=15.5 MeV
Thermal neutrons were obtained by slowing down the fast
neutrons in polyethylene block; Cd ratio ~ 16 .
Coincidence.
For each neutron energy the measurements were done
alternately with short runs (~ 1 hr) with thermal and
fast neutrons.
Silicon detector for counting of fission fragments
The geometrical efficiency of FF detection about 30 %.
For determination of mass-yield and kinetic energy
of fission fragment for compound nucleus U-235
the results of following articles were used-
1.G.P.Ford,r.B.Leachman,Phys.Rev.,137,B826,1965
2. V.M.Pankratov,V.M.Strutinskij, AE,14,171,1963
3. V.I.Senchenko et al. YF,5,514,1967
Method of correction fro prompt neutrons.
NU as function of fragment mass at U-235 thermal
neutron fission was used in analysis.
The U235 target was a thin layer of uranyl nitrate
(enriched to ~90% in U-235) of thickness 30 mu-g/cm2
and diam. 2 cm, deposited on a gold-coated organic
film ~10 mu-g/cm2 thick by evaporation of an alcohol
solution of uranyl nitrate in an electric field.
Energy scale was calibrated by measurements with
thermal neutrons.
Fission fragments
It was assumed that deltaNU=NUfast-NUthermal is
distributed between the fragments in proportion to
their masses. NU - from BNL-325. NU as function of
FF mass at thermal fission - from REL-REF of V.Apalin+.
Following corrections were taken into account
1. Sheppard correction
2. correction connected with irradiation of detector
by prompt neutrons
3. on motion of centre-of-mass
4. geometry efficiency of counting fission
fragments by detectors is equal 30%.
Further measurement ( different
INC-SOURCE).
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Ref. on Engl.transl. of YF added. ANALYSIS -> REL-REF
SAMPLE was added. ERR-ANALYS moved to Subent 2.
M.M.
TKE
Average mass of heavy fission fragment.
Variance of average kinetic energy
of fission fragments. Dimension - MeV**2.
Dispersion of HF average mass distribution,
units - amu**2.
Error of measurements is determined mainly
by statistical error
error of quantity MISC1
Table 1 of J,SNP,8,165,1969
= Table 1 of ICD-4,286
SD: MISC3 -> MISC1-ERR
M.M. "SEE TEXT" -> MEV-SQ.
PRE was added in SF5, FF -> LF+HF .
MISC2 -> DATA 2 (REACTION 2) .
MISC4 was added.
